title: dryopteris chrysodioides
intro: Dryopteris chrysodioides, also known as the golden wood fern, is a species of fern native to eastern Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its bright green fronds and golden-brown scales on the underside of its leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dryopteris chrysodioides are said to include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, making it a popular choice in traditional remedies. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea, infused in oil for topical use, or added to tinctures to support its medicinal properties.
